message:
refactor: migrate test_e2e_robot_docs.sh to test_e2e_framework.sh (bd-89zv)

Replace ad-hoc test infrastructure (counters, colors, setup/cleanup,
assertions) with standardized e2e framework. All 49 assertions still pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -226,7 +226,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-810","title":"Handle network timeouts gracefully","description":"**Handle network timeouts gracefully**

## What
Add timeout handling for clone and pull operations.

## Why
Flaky networks, slow connections, or overloaded servers can cause operations to hang indefinitely. Users need a way to bound wait time.

## Implementation
- Set GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_LIMIT=1000 and GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_TIME=30 as defaults
- These cause git to abort if transfer rate drops below 1KB/s for 30 seconds
- Add --timeout N option to override (sets GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_TIME)
- On timeout, log error and continue to next repo
- Report timeouts distinctly in summary (not just 'failed')

## Acceptance Criteria
- Slow operations time out after reasonable period
- Timeout errors are clearly reported
- Other repos continue processing after timeout
- Timeout can be configured via flag or config","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T21:10:05.881422408Z","updated_at":"2026-01-03T22:01:47.558978485Z","closed_at":"2026-01-03T22:01:47.558978485Z","close_reason":"Implemented network timeout handling with GIT_HTTP_LOW_SPEED_* env vars, --timeout flag, and timeout detection in clone/pull","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"labels":["resilience"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-810","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T21:14:13.921445046Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-810","depends_on_id":"bd-806","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T21:14:13.952318959Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-810","depends_on_id":"bd-807","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T21:14:13.981841826Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-895v","title":"Audit caam (Coding Agent Account Manager) for package distribution","description":"# Audit: caam (Coding Agent Account Manager) for Package Distribution

## Tool Overview

**Repository**: /data/projects/coding_agent_account_manager (https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_account_manager)
**Language**: Go (assumed based on "Coming Soon via GoReleaser" note)
**Purpose**: Switch between AI coding agent accounts/configurations

caam helps developers who use multiple AI coding agent accounts:
- Switch between different Claude Code accounts
- Manage multiple Cursor configurations
- Handle API key rotation
- Maintain separate agent identities for different projects/clients

## Audit Checklist

### 1. Binary Distribution Readiness
- [ ] Verify project language (Go expected)
- [ ] Check for existing GitHub releases
- [ ] Check if releases include pre-built binaries
- [ ] Identify supported platforms

### 2. GoReleaser Status
- [ ] Check for existing .goreleaser.yml
- [ ] If missing, flag for GoReleaser setup
- [ ] Check for release automation

### 3. CLI Interface
- [ ] Verify CLI entry point exists
- [ ] Check for standard flags (--version, --help)
- [ ] Document main commands/subcommands

### 4. Security Considerations
- [ ] Note how credentials/API keys are handled
- [ ] Check for secure storage mechanisms
- [ ] Document any keychain/credential manager integration

## Platform Considerations

Account management tool should ideally work on all platforms where AI coding agents run:
- macOS: Primary development platform for many
- Linux: Server and developer workstations
- Windows: Growing AI coding agent user base

## Use Case Context

Developers increasingly use multiple AI coding agent subscriptions:
- Personal vs work accounts
- Different API providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.)
- Client-specific configurations

caam addresses the pain of manual config switching.

## Expected Outcome

Determine if ready for packaging or needs GoReleaser/development work.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-14T03:23:38.254274748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-14T04:11:54.826277228Z","closed_at":"2026-01-14T04:11:54.826277228Z","close_reason":"AUDIT COMPLETE: caam is fully ready for package distribution.

FINDINGS:
✓ GoReleaser: Config at .goreleaser.yaml with multi-platform builds (Linux/Darwin/Windows), Homebrew brews, Scoop manifests
✓ Releases: v0.1.2 is latest (Jan 14, 2026) with all binaries
✓ Secrets: HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN set
✓ Homebrew: Formula at Formula/caam.rb, auto-generated by GoReleaser
✓ Scoop: Manifest at scoop-bucket/caam.json, auto-generated by GoReleaser
✓ CLI: Has --version, shell completions

REMAINING: README needs Homebrew/Scoop installation instructions (bd-mw0f)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0}

 {"id":"bd-8bxp","title":"Implement validate_commit_plan() function","description":"# Commit Plan Validation

## Parent Epic: bd-jk4n (Security Guardrails & Validation)

## Purpose
Validate agent-produced commit plans BEFORE execution. This is the critical security gate.

## Implementation

```bash
# Validate commit plan before execution
# Args: $1=commit_plan_json, $2=repo_path
# Returns: 0=valid, 1=blocked (sets VALIDATION_ERROR)
# Sets: VALIDATION_WARNINGS array for non-blocking issues
validate_commit_plan() {
    local plan="$1"
    local repo_path="$2"
    VALIDATION_ERROR=""
    VALIDATION_WARNINGS=()
    
    log_verbose "Validating commit plan..."
    
    # 1. Validate plan structure
    if \! json_validate "$plan"; then
        VALIDATION_ERROR="Invalid JSON structure in commit plan"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # 2. Check for required fields
    local commits push
    commits=$(json_get_field "$plan" "commits")
    push=$(json_get_field "$plan" "push")
    
    if [[ -z "$commits" ]] || [[ "$commits" == "null" ]]; then
        VALIDATION_ERROR="Missing or empty commits array in plan"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # 3. Validate each commit entry
    local commit_count=0
    while IFS= read -r commit_json; do
        ((commit_count++))
        
        local files message
        files=$(json_get_field "$commit_json" "files")
        message=$(json_get_field "$commit_json" "message")
        
        # Check commit has files
        if [[ -z "$files" ]] || [[ "$files" == "null" ]] || [[ "$files" == "[]" ]]; then
            VALIDATION_ERROR="Commit $commit_count has no files"
            return 1
        fi
        
        # Check commit has message
        if [[ -z "$message" ]]; then
            VALIDATION_ERROR="Commit $commit_count has no message"
            return 1
        fi
        
        # 4. Validate each file
        for file in $(echo "$files" | tr -d "[]\"" | tr "," "\n"); do
            file=$(echo "$file" | xargs)  # Trim whitespace
            [[ -z "$file" ]] && continue
            
            # Check against denylist
            if is_file_denied "$file"; then
                VALIDATION_ERROR="Denied file in commit $commit_count: $file"
                log_error "BLOCKED: $file matches denylist pattern"
                return 1
            fi
            
            # Check file exists (warning only for missing)
            if [[ \! -f "$repo_path/$file" ]] && [[ \! -e "$repo_path/$file" ]]; then
                VALIDATION_WARNINGS+=("File not found: $file (will be skipped)")
            fi
            
            # Check file size
            if [[ -f "$repo_path/$file" ]]; then
                if is_file_too_large "$repo_path/$file"; then
                    local size_mb
                    size_mb=$(get_file_size_mb "$repo_path/$file")
                    VALIDATION_ERROR="File too large: $file (${size_mb}MB > ${AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_FILE_MB}MB limit)"
                    return 1
                fi
                
                # Check for binaries
                if is_binary_file "$repo_path/$file"; then
                    if \! is_binary_allowed "$file"; then
                        VALIDATION_ERROR="Binary file not allowed: $file"
                        return 1
                    else
                        VALIDATION_WARNINGS+=("Binary file included: $file (explicitly allowed)")
                    fi
                fi
            fi
        done
    done <<< "$(echo "$commits" | jq -c ".[]" 2>/dev/null || echo "$commits")"
    
    # 5. Check for too many commits (sanity check)
    if [[ "$commit_count" -gt "${AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_COMMITS:-50}" ]]; then
        VALIDATION_ERROR="Too many commits in plan: $commit_count (max ${AGENT_SWEEP_MAX_COMMITS:-50})"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # 6. Run secret scan on staged changes simulation
    if [[ "$AGENT_SWEEP_SECRET_SCAN" \!= "off" ]]; then
        if \! run_secret_scan "$repo_path"; then
            VALIDATION_ERROR="Secrets detected in changes: $SCAN_FINDINGS"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    
    # Log warnings
    for warn in "${VALIDATION_WARNINGS[@]}"; do
        log_warn "  ⚠ $warn"
    done
    
    log_verbose "Commit plan validated: $commit_count commits, push=$push"
    return 0
}
```

## Validation Checks Summary
1. Valid JSON structure
2. Required fields present (commits array, message per commit)
3. Files not on denylist
4. Files under size limit
5. No unexpected binaries
6. Commit count sanity check
7. Secret scan passes

## Error Reporting
- VALIDATION_ERROR: Fatal error message (blocks execution)
- VALIDATION_WARNINGS: Non-blocking warnings array (logged)

## Integration Point
Called AFTER plan extraction, BEFORE execute_commit_plan().","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T22:18:28.735917300Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T04:00:14.106658771Z","closed_at":"2026-01-07T04:00:14.106658771Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8bxp","depends_on_id":"bd-0ghe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T22:27:16.871397233Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-8bxp","depends_on_id":"bd-5iwb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T22:27:16.894997273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-8bxp","depends_on_id":"bd-nqjy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T22:27:16.842005701Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

-    printf "${GREEN}  PASS${RESET} %s\n" "$1"

-

-    printf "${RED}  FAIL${RESET} %s\n" "$1"

-    fi

-echo ""
-echo "=================================="
-printf "Results: ${GREEN}%d passed${RESET}, ${RED}%d failed${RESET}\n" "$TESTS_PASSED" "$TESTS_FAILED"
-echo "=================================="
-
-if [[ "$TESTS_FAILED" -gt 0 ]]; then
-    exit 1
-fi
-exit 0
+run_test test_valid_json_all_topics
+run_test test_envelope_structure
+run_test test_schema_version
+run_test test_all_topic_includes_sections
+run_test test_commands_topic_coverage
+run_test test_exit_codes_coverage
+run_test test_invalid_topic
+run_test test_default_topic_is_all
+run_test test_schemas_has_command_schemas
+run_test test_schema_shortcut
+run_test test_version_matches
+
+print_results
